km_name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Pollution, Chemicals, Air and Soil
purpose: Binary Yes/No SpecificTopic decisions for the pollution_chemicals_air_soil family. This simplified KM owns its tag-level reconciliation and emits only final Yes tags for this family.

rules: Answer every tag in this KM independently as Yes or No before producing the family final list.
Emit only tags answered Yes in final_specific_topics_from_this_family.
Do not emit candidates, centrality labels, or nearby labels as final tags.
If two tags overlap, keep both only when the source has distinct operative evidence for both; otherwise answer Yes only for the more directly satisfied decision question.
Reject incidental, background, implementation-actor-only, and keyword-only evidence even when a semantic signal appears.
The standalone SpecificTopic reconciler has been removed; this family KM is responsible for suppressing weak/adjacent labels before code consolidation.
weak_evidence_policy: Weak or not-observed tags remain in the enum, but need direct source evidence at runtime.
Do not use weak master examples as independent proof; use them only as cautionary boundary tests.

tag_decisions.tag: canonical SpecificTopic value from this KM
tag_decisions.evidence_excerpt: required when answer is Yes; empty string allowed when No
tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional canonical tag that better fits the evidence

final_specific_topics_from_this_family: all tags from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, already reconciled by this family KM

tags.definition: Controls, objectives, monitoring, or improvement measures for air pollution and the quality of outdoor air.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Air Quality"?
yes_when: The instrument centrally regulates emissions, pollutants, monitoring, plans, or duties intended to protect or improve air quality.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The text only mentions air as one environmental medium in a broad law, or the central rule is climate-only greenhouse gas accounting without air pollutant controls.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "on ambient air quality and cleaner air for Europe". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Air Quality"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The instrument centrally regulates emissions, pollutants, monitoring, plans, or duties intended to protect or improve air quality.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "air quality" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The instrument centrally regulates emissions, pollutants, monitoring, plans, or duties intended to protect or improve air quality.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Air Quality"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The text only mentions air as one environmental medium in a broad law, or the central rule is climate-only greenhouse gas accounting without air pollutant controls.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Air Quality"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "on the fees and charges payable to the European Chemicals Agency pursuant to Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 on the Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH)". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Chemical Regulation"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source establishes chemical regulatory schemes such as REACH/CLP/PIC, chemical registers, authorization, restrictions, test methods, or data-sharing obligations.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "REACH" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source establishes chemical regulatory schemes such as REACH/CLP/PIC, chemical registers, authorization, restrictions, test methods, or data-sharing obligations.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Chemical Regulation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source only addresses safety outcomes or hazardous-substance exposure without regulatory scheme mechanics.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Chemical Regulation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "An Act to make provision in relation to corporations and financial products and services, and for other purposes". Standing alone, should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Chemical Regulation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.

tags.definition: Dangerous, toxic, hazardous, radioactive, carcinogenic, mutagenic, ozone-depleting, or otherwise harmful substances/materials.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Hazardous Substances"?
yes_when: The source centrally regulates dangerous chemicals, hazardous waste, POPs, asbestos, silica, radioactive waste, ozone-depleting substances, dangerous pollutants, or hazardous substance restrictions.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: Do not tag merely because a product, facility, or environmental law has potential hazards; require hazardous substances or dangerous pollutants to be central.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "establishing a list of hazardous waste pursuant to Article 1(4) of Council Directive 91/689/EEC on hazardous waste". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Hazardous Substances"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source centrally regulates dangerous chemicals, hazardous waste, POPs, asbestos, silica, radioactive waste, ozone-depleting substances, dangerous pollutants, or hazardous substance restrictions.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Hazardous Substances"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "Do not tag merely because a product, facility, or environmental law has potential hazards; require hazardous substances or dangerous pollutants to be central.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Hazardous Substances"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

tags.definition: Limits, bans, or restrictions on hazardous substances in products, materials, equipment, or supply chains.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Restriction of Hazardous Substances"?
yes_when: The source sets substance restrictions such as RoHS, banned/limited hazardous substances in electrical/electronic equipment, or product material restrictions.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source broadly regulates hazardous substances without product/content restriction limits.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

tags.definition: Authorization, approval, registration, renewal, or market access for biocidal products and active substances.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Registration of Biocidal Products"?
yes_when: The source directly regulates registration/authorization of biocidal products, active substances, dossiers, approvals, or market placement.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The source is broad chemical regulation or pesticide regulation without biocidal product registration.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.
Do not answer Yes from analogy to weak master examples, broad ESG relatedness, a title-only hunch, or the absence of a better label.
